title: Hedgemony Digital Example Scenario
learning_objectives: Explore posture, readiness, hedging, and resource tradeoffs.
Exercise Blue free-play adjudication and Red signaled-card sequencing.
Demonstrate deterministic tables, card-defined outcomes, and White Cell pauses.

players.label: United States
players.victory_condition: IPs > IPs of everyone, and DPRK does not win
players.notes: U.S. readiness is deliberately split by readiness level so printed readiness tables apply without interpolation.

players.label: NATO/EU
players.victory_condition: IPs > IPs of RU, and RU does not win

players.label: China
players.victory_condition: IPs >= US IPs - 3, and DPRK neither wins nor loses

players.label: North Korea
players.victory_condition: IPs > 15, or U.S. leaves Korean Peninsula; lose if IPs = 0

victory_conditions.US: IPs > IPs of everyone, and DPRK does not win
victory_conditions.NATO_EU: IPs > IPs of RU, and RU does not win
victory_conditions.PRC: IPs >= US IPs - 3, and DPRK neither wins nor loses
victory_conditions.DPRK: IPs > 15, or U.S. leaves Korean Peninsula; lose if IPs = 0

US.title: Integrated C4ISR Upgrade
US.description: Invest in C4ISR integration. In the example scenario this upgrades the U.S. C4ISR critical capability by one level if the National Tech Level cap allows it.

US.title: Procurement Surge
US.description: Use the procurement table through the dedicated engine command. The sample UI buys 1 FF at M3 and 90% readiness so the next readiness bill remains inside printed sustainment rows.

value.note: Use procureForces command for exact FF and Mod Level.

US.title: Freedom of Navigation Operation
US.description: Resolve as an RT B noncombat posture interaction.

US.title: Blue Free-Play Initiative
US.description: Blue articulates intent; the White Cell translates it into Hedgemony game terms.

US.title: Procure Weapons to Ukraine
US.description: Blue procures weapons to Ukraine, increasing Russia's chance to lose the war starting next turn.
US.notes: If one Blue player procures weapons, Russia loses the Ukraine war on 8-9. If both Blue players procure weapons, Russia loses on 7-9.

US.title: Attack on Critical Infrastructure
US.description: A player attacks critical infrastructure. Targets are assigned by the White Cell.
US.notes: White Cell applies target IP and RP-per-turn effects after targets are selected.

US.title: Test SM-3 IIA on ICBM Target
US.description: The U.S. tests SM-3 Block IIA against an ICBM target.
US.notes: RU or PRC response testing is handled as a follow-on RT B or White Cell adjudication.

US.title: Unilateral Iran Sanctions
US.description: The United States sanctions Iran unilaterally and breaks the JCPOA treaty.
US.notes: Continuing every-other-turn sanctions effects are tracked for White Cell timing.

NATO_EU.title: Host-Nation Access Package
NATO_EU.description: Creates a scenario flag for improved EUCOM access.

NATO_EU.title: EUCOM Readiness Exercise
NATO_EU.description: Resolve as RT B against Russian posture.

RU.title: Limited Incursion
RU.description: A force-on-force action resolved on CRT A.

RU.title: Political Warfare
RU.description: A private action resolved with a card-defined D10 table.

RU.title: Long-Range Fires Modernization
RU.description: Attempts to improve RU LRF; capped by National Tech Level.
RU.notes: The example leaves LRF unchanged because RU is already at its National Tech Level.

RU.title: Procure New Forces
RU.description: Does not count against Red per-turn card costs or limits; pay only procurement table cost through the dedicated command.

RU.title: Send Resources to Propaganda in the US
RU.description: Russia supports a pro-Russian news network in the United States.

RU.title: Procure Weapons from a Rogue State
RU.description: Russia may buy drones from Iran, ammunition from DPRK, or both.
RU.notes: White Cell records the chosen seller, extra 3 RP payment, transferred FFs, and next Ukraine-war roll modifier.

RU.title: Use Human Waves
RU.description: Russia uses human waves to capture a town.
RU.notes: Use equal FF commitments to resolve this as RT B Parity.

RU.title: Strengthen Proxies in the Region
RU.description: Russia sends modest support to proxies in up to three selected areas.
RU.notes: White Cell confirms areas and exact RP/IP count.

RU.title: Joint Military Exercises with Partners
RU.description: Russia conducts joint military exercises with partners.
RU.notes: Partner agreement and partner IP gain are White Cell confirmed.

RU.title: Cooperate with U.S. on Arms Control Treaty
RU.description: U.S. and Russia may agree to limit forces in designated European countries.
RU.notes: Agreement and force limits are White Cell confirmed before effects are applied.

RU.title: Cyber Attacks During an Election
RU.description: Russia conducts cyber attacks against major political parties during a national election.

PRC.title: Maritime Pressure Campaign
PRC.description: Resolve as RT B against Blue presence.

PRC.title: Crisis Escalation
PRC.description: A CRT A action in INDOPACOM.

PRC.title: Out-of-Area Logistics Node
PRC.description: Creates a base marker with a one-RP out-of-area discount for PRC in CENTCOM.

PRC.title: Sell US Foreign Reserves
PRC.description: China sells U.S. foreign reserves in response to U.S. pressure.

prerequisites.description: May be used as a response to U.S. action or pressure against PRC.

PRC.notes: The response trigger is a prerequisite recorded for White Cell confirmation.

PRC.subtype: Build Permanent Base in another AOR
PRC.title: Build Permanent Base in Another AOR
PRC.description: Builds a PRC permanent base outside INDOPACOM, reducing out-of-area operation cost in that AOR.

play_constraints.frequency: May be played once every three turns; cannot play more than once per AOR

PRC.title: Procure New Forces
PRC.description: Procure new PRC forces at any mod level up to National Tech Level using the procurement table.

value.note: Use procureForces command for exact FF and mod level.

PRC.title: Modernize Existing Forces
PRC.description: Upgrade up to 20% of PRC FFs by one mod level using the modernization table.

value.note: Use modernizeForces command for selected forces.

PRC.title: Upgrade LRF Targeting of Maritime Targets (C4ISR)
PRC.description: PRC upgrades C4ISR for maritime LRF targeting.

play_constraints.frequency: May play once per two turns

PRC.title: Invest in R&D to Increase National Tech Level by 1
PRC.description: PRC invests in R&D to increase National Tech Level.
PRC.notes: Current generic rules do not branch on tech-lead status, so the non-leading printed row is encoded and tech-lead confirmation remains a White Cell note.

PRC.title: Gray Zone Pressure on INDOPACOM Non-U.S.-Ally
PRC.description: PRC applies gray-zone pressure on a non-U.S. ally in INDOPACOM.
PRC.notes: If unopposed, White Cell applies U.S. -1 IP and Red Advantage column.

PRC.title: Silk Road Economic Belt
PRC.description: China attempts to gain influence through the Silk Road Economic Belt.
PRC.notes: Partnering with Russia is recorded as a card-defined player choice.

PRC.title: SCS Expansion: Reclaim and Occupy Another SCS Island
PRC.description: PRC expands a South China Sea island position.
PRC.notes: The printed completion turn depends on the D10 row; the generic scheduler records the delayed IP gain for White Cell timing.

DPRK.title: Nuclear Forces Development
DPRK.description: Private investment visible to DPRK and White Cell.

DPRK.title: DPRK Ransomware Attack (Minor)
DPRK.description: DPRK performs a cyber attack on infrastructure; targets and recovery are adjudicated by the White Cell.

play_constraints.frequency: May play once per two turns

DPRK.notes: White Cell adjudicates affected target(s), ransom, recovery roll, and maintenance.

DPRK.title: DPRK Ransomware Attack (Major)
DPRK.description: DPRK performs a major cyber attack on infrastructure-related IT systems.

play_constraints.frequency: May play once per two turns

DPRK.notes: White Cell adjudicates affected target(s), ransom, recovery roll, and maintenance.

DPRK.title: Malware Injection Army
DPRK.description: DPRK invests in malware support for its next ransomware attack.

DPRK.title: Ransomware as a Service
DPRK.description: DPRK offers another player a ransomware attack opportunity.
DPRK.notes: White Cell selects the buyer, records DPRK's 1 FF loss, and blocks DPRK ransomware attacks this turn.

IR.title: Strait Harassment
IR.description: Resolve as RT B in CENTCOM.

IR.title: Proxy Mobilization
IR.description: Roll proxy reliability before resolving participation.

IR.title: Proxy Force Development
IR.description: Use proxy development command for exact sponsor share and reliability.

IR.title: Iran Provocation (MRBM Test)
IR.description: Iran conducts an MRBM test; U.S. response can escalate to RT B.
IR.notes: If U.S. responds, resolve the printed RT B response through White Cell or a separate response resolution.

IR.title: Gray Zone Pressure on GCC State
IR.description: Iran applies gray-zone pressure on a GCC state.

US.title: Interceptor Test Series Fails (IAMD)
US.description: Unsuccessful tests delay planned IAMD upgrades.

US.title: Advance in Self-Driving Cars
US.description: Commercial AI advances reduce U.S. force modernization costs.
US.notes: The 25% per-level discount is represented as a scenario flag for the modernization command.

RU.title: Prigozhin Starts His Mutiny
RU.description: The Wagner leader has started his rebellion.

RU.title: Intelligence Coup
RU.description: Turkish officers share classified NATO technology with Russia.

PRC.title: Successful Party Congress
PRC.description: China conducts a successful Party Congress and reduces selected regional pressure/incursion costs for two turns.
PRC.notes: Cost discounts are recorded as a scenario flag for card cost adjudication.

PRC.title: Inadvertent Escalation in South China Sea
PRC.description: A coast guard clash damages Chinese soft power and economic-development rolls.

PRC.title: Research Success (C4ISR)
PRC.description: Chinese space research improves prospects for the next C4ISR upgrade.

PRC.title: Research Failure (LRF)
PRC.description: Problems with PRC LRF research block LRF upgrades for two turns.

DPRK.title: Kim Dies: Coup
DPRK.description: U.S. and PRC may respond to a DPRK leadership coup; responder deploys forces and rolls on the printed table.
DPRK.notes: Existing rules support this as White Cell adjudication because leadership, no-response fallback, and four-turn pinning are player-choice dependent.

DPRK.title: China Tech Assist
DPRK.description: China provides missile technology assistance to DPRK.
DPRK.notes: Applies +1 to the next DPRK missile test die roll.

IR.title: Revolutionary Guard Coup
IR.description: The Revolutionary Guard starts a coup after a lack of reaction to events in Israel.

IR.title: Support to Hezbollah
IR.description: Iran gains influence from additional Hezbollah support, but expends resources next turn.

IR.title: Terror Comes to Tehran
IR.description: Terror attacks in Tehran shape Iranian incentives for one turn and spook investors.
IR.notes: White Cell applies the +2 IP conditional bonus based on Iran attribution and force use.

NATO_EU.title: Sweden and Finland Join NATO
NATO_EU.description: Finland and Sweden join NATO after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

NATO_EU.title: Interoperability Improves C4ISR
NATO_EU.description: NATO increases interoperability with U.S. systems and benefits from improved C4ISR.
NATO_EU.notes: White Cell confirms the printed cap that NATO C4ISR cannot exceed U.S. C4ISR.

NATO_EU.title: Unable to Reach Policy Consensus
NATO_EU.description: Turkey opposes U.S. support to Kurdish fighters in Syria, causing friction within NATO.
NATO_EU.notes: The flag blocks NATO repositioning next action phase while still allowing responses.

international_event.title: Global Market Shock
international_event.description: A public international event with resource effects.

international_event.title: Ambiguous Regional Crisis
international_event.description: The White Cell defines who is affected and how.

international_event.title: Attack on Critical Infrastructure
international_event.description: Critical infrastructure has been disabled under strange circumstances and appears sabotaged.
international_event.notes: White Cell identifies affected player(s) and may resolve the optional shaming D10 table.

international_event.title: Hamas-Israel Conflict
international_event.description: Hamas launches a surprise attack on Israel from Gaza. Israel seeks retaliation.
international_event.notes: White Cell handles Blue force placement, Israel brutality RT A/RT B style adjudication, optional Iran proxy activation, U.S. entry, and Russian PMC entry.

international_event.title: Boko Haram
international_event.description: Boko Haram occupies Kano and advances on other major cities.
international_event.notes: U.S. intervention choice, Nigeria CF purchase, CRT A commitments, and pinning are resolved by White Cell using existing CRT A rules.

international_event.title: South Sudanese Genocide
international_event.description: Peacekeeping response to escalating genocide in South Sudan.

modifiers.description: +2 per U.S., NATO/EU, or Chinese FF sent to South Sudan

international_event.notes: Use args.modifier for the +2 per committed FF modifier; player-specific peacekeeper IP awards are White Cell confirmed.

international_event.title: Finding Kony
international_event.description: The LRA renews violence and prompts calls to kill or capture Joseph Kony.
international_event.notes: U.S. support spending, up-to-four-turn repeated search rolls, and no-spend penalty are White Cell adjudicated.

marker_chits.label: Pinned 1 Turn
marker_chits.description: Committed forces are tied to the AOI for one turn.

marker_chits.label: Pinned 2 Turns
marker_chits.description: Committed forces are tied to the AOI for two turns.

marker_chits.label: Reset Required
marker_chits.description: Forces return home and must reset before use.
